accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and the SBA/SBIC context (privately owned firms licensed by SBA) is accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the SBA administration and SBIC naming are correct real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose: amending the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to change SBIC leverage rules and exclusions.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies that the bill amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958, including the private capital definition and leverage caps.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the government-funds exception, the 50%/$125 million cap, and the prospective-only eligibility limit.

effective_dates.why: Notes prospective applicability after enactment and the May 19, 2026 approval date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the private capital redefinition, restructured leverage caps, and broadened exclusion for rural/critical-tech/small-manufacturer investments.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive throughout with no editorializing.

concise.why: Compact and free of padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names SBA, SBICs, the 1958 Act, title 10's covered technology category, and section 301(c).

correct_figures.why: All figures ($300M→$200M, $250M, $175M, $350M→$475M/$350M, 50%, $125M) match the bill text.
